bead.title: bench: preflight smoke test for each lane in run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh
bead.description:
Add a per-lane preflight check to scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh that fires a single 1-token chat completion against each requested lane's endpoint before any matrix runner spawns. If the endpoint cannot produce a token within 30s using the lane's actual sampling params, fail the sweep with a non-zero exit and a clear message naming the lane, the endpoint, the model id, and the underlying error.

Why this matters: see parent epic. The 2026-05-10 sindri-llamacpp incident wasted 119 cells (~93 min) on a `reasoning: low` parameter the llama-server provider rejects. The same hour, sindri-vllm wasted multiple cells on a hung model engine where /v1/models responded but /v1/chat/completions never streamed tokens. A single curl-to-completion check per lane catches all these classes (plus auth failures, missing-model 404s, connection-refused, wrong content-type) at sweep startup, before any matrix runner spawns.

Behavior:

- Fire after `Resolved tasks:` is printed, before the per-lane "starting" markers.
- For each lane in the resolved plan: build a JSON body using the lane's `fizeau_env.FIZEAU_MODEL` and the lane's sampling block (temperature, top_p, top_k, reasoning when present). POST to `<base_url>/chat/completions` with `max_tokens: 1` and a 30s timeout.
- Pass condition: the response is JSON-parseable and `.choices[0].message.content` is a non-null string (even an empty string is OK — the model started decoding).
- Fail condition: any non-2xx, timeout, malformed JSON, or missing message content. Print the lane id, base_url, model id, sampling params attempted, and the response body (truncated to 1KB).
- Skip preflight for managed-cloud lanes that have always-on SLAs (openrouter, openai, anthropic) — these are well-known and a preflight just burns quota. Identify by checking the resource group's `provider_type` against an allow-list (vllm, llama-server, omlx, lmstudio, rapid-mlx, ollama, lucebox).

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — add preflight_lane() function + per-lane invocation loop right before the existing per-lane plan output.
- scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml — no schema change needed; the existing `fizeau_env` and `sampling` blocks are sufficient.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Pre-resume validation that the model server is the same version as last time (different problem; would need version stamps on cells).
- Per-cell health probe during the sweep (defense-in-depth piece is fizeau-XXXX, the consecutive-failure halt bead).
- Adding the smoke check to non-TerminalBench benchmark scripts.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With sindri-llamacpp configured to use `reasoning: low` (the broken state we just fixed), `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"` (or equivalent error text) and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. With sindri-llamacpp correctly configured (no `reasoning` param), the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. Test by killing the llama-server process: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` (or curl error 7) and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. The cloud lane skip works: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call openrouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.

Cost budget: at most ~30s × N_lanes added to sweep startup wall time. For an 11-lane full sweep that's ~5 min, much cheaper than waiting for the first cell of each broken lane to fail.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestPreflightLaneMisconfiguredReasoning` covers sindri-llamacpp configured with `reasoning: low`: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero in under 60s with a `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED` message naming the unsupported reasoning parameter and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. `TestPreflightLaneOK` covers correctly configured sindri-llamacpp: the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. `TestPreflightLaneConnectionRefused` covers stopped llama-server: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` or curl error 7, and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. `TestPreflightManagedCloudSkipped` covers cloud lanes: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call OpenRouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.
5. `scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash` or an equivalent shellspec file defines the four test cases above against a mock HTTP server.

events.body: Bead reasoning was sound — the worker's intake just flagged acceptance criteria changes during pre-claim due to a noisy worktree (stale .ddx/executions files). That root cause is now resolved (the cleanup committed as 9d77b81a). Bead description and acceptance criteria are still valid; retry the implementation. Recent live incident on 2026-05-11 confirmed the value: vidar oMLX endpoint went down and 70 cells silently failed before detection — exactly what this preflight check would have caught in <30s.
Operator reviewed intake block; retry unchanged to keep worker queue moving.
Operator normalized acceptance into explicit numbered criteria from the existing description; retry with corrected acceptance.
Operator corrected acceptance and restarted worker with stronger pre-claim model after repeated gpt-5.4-mini schema failures; retry.
Operator disabled intake rewrites in bead-lifecycle skill and acceptance is already explicit; retry.
Operator patched local DDx pre-claim intake to stop requesting rewrites; retry.

Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata; retry.
